title: 24.6 — Adding new functionality to a derived class

content.text: This is because there is no getValue() function in Base. Function getValue() belongs to Derived. Because Derived is a Base, Derived has access to stuff in Base. However, Base does not have access to anything in Derived.Next lesson24.7Calling inherited functions and overriding behaviorBack to table of contentsPrevious lesson24.5Inheritance and access specifiers
